mnemonics.title: Green IT Pillars
mnemonics.content: The 3 E's: Environment, Energy, Economics - balancing sustainability, efficiency, and cost-effectiveness

mnemonics.title: E-waste Hazards
mnemonics.content: LEAD: Lead, Mercury, Arsenic, Cadmium, Dioxins - toxic materials in electronics

acronyms.usage: Measures data center energy efficiency - lower is better (ideal ~1.1)
EU directive limiting toxic materials in electronics
EU directive for proper e-waste disposal and recycling
Rating system for electronic product environmental impact
All digital technology for information handling
Manufacturers responsible for end-of-life product disposal
Gases contributing to global warming (CO2, CH4, N2O)

acronyms.expansion: Creating virtual resources from physical hardware

visualTips.visualization: Think of PUE as a fraction: Total Energy (numerator) / IT Energy (denominator). Imagine a pie chart where the 'slice' outside computing is overhead - smaller slice = better PUE
Picture a pizza cut into multiple slices - one physical server becomes multiple virtual servers. Each slice works independently but shares the same crust (hardware)
Think of a boomerang: Raw Materials → Manufacturing → Usage → Disposal → Recycling → Raw Materials (circular, sustainable)
Imagine a scale balancing IT operations on one side and trees on the other - Green IT aims to make the IT side lighter
Think of moving from a house of many small rooms (many servers) to an apartment building (consolidated servers) - same people, less space and energy

analogies.analogy: PUE is like fuel efficiency in a car - lower numbers mean less 'wasted' energy reaching your destination (computing work)
Virtualization is like a magic show - one box (stage) appears to contain many different acts (virtual machines) performing simultaneously
Thin clients are like information booths - they just display what the main office (server) provides, using minimal power themselves
Cloud computing is like a power grid - instead of every home having its own generator, everyone shares one massive efficient power plant
E-waste recycling is like urban mining - extracting valuable and toxic materials from discarded 'ore' (old electronics)
Green IT policy is like a diet plan for organizations - setting rules and targets to become more 'fit' and sustainable
